explanation: The natural language statement specifies a bounded invariance property, meaning that the condition 'angular roll accelerations greater than the capability' must not persist for longer than 10 time units. This implies that if this condition becomes true, it must become false within the next 10 time units. This is accurately captured by the LTL formula G (F[0,N_DURATION] !bool_exp3), which states that 'Globally, eventually within 0 to N_DURATION time units, bool_exp3 must be false'. To achieve this, we use template 14: (G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))). We set bool_exp2 to 'true' because the constraint is universal (not conditional on another event). We define bool_exp3 as '!angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' because the property requires the *absence* of excessive acceleration within the specified duration. N_DURATION is set to 10 as per the natural language.

The property states that it is globally true that if 'angular roll accelerations greater than the capability' (bool_exp2) occur at any point, then within a duration of 10 time units (N_DURATION) from that point (inclusive of the current state), the condition must become false (bool_exp3, which is the negation of bool_exp2). This accurately captures the requirement that the specified angular roll accelerations shall not persist for durations exceeding 10 time units.

The property asserts that at any given time, if 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' is true, then it must become false within the current time unit or the next 10 time units. This ensures that the condition 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' does not remain true for a total duration exceeding 10 time units.

The natural language statement implies that a specific condition ('angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability') should never be true for an extended period, specifically for durations exceeding 10 time units. This can be translated into a LTL property stating that globally, if the condition 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' becomes true, it must eventually become false within a bounded future. Template 14, (G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))), perfectly captures this requirement. Here, 'bool_exp2' represents the onset of the condition we want to limit, and 'bool_exp3' represents the negation of that condition, ensuring it eventually ceases within 'N_DURATION' steps. Setting N_DURATION to 10 ensures that the condition cannot persist for 11 or more consecutive time units, thus preventing durations exceeding 10 time units.

The natural language statement "Shall not command X for durations exceeding N time units" can be translated to the LTL property that states "Globally, it is eventually true that X is false, within the next N time units (inclusive of the current time unit)." This ensures that X cannot persist for N+1 or more consecutive time units. We use 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' as the property P, and N_DURATION is set to 10 as per the input. The LTL formula becomes G (F[0,10] !P). This form is captured by Template 14 when bool_exp2 is set to a tautology (always true) and bool_exp3 is set to !P.

The property states that a condition (angular roll accelerations greater than the system's capability) should not hold for a duration exceeding 10 time units. This can be translated into a LTL property that asserts: globally, if the condition becomes true, it must eventually become false within 10 time units. This is captured by the template 'G (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))' where bool_exp2 is the condition that should not persist, and bool_exp3 is the negation of that condition, and N_DURATION is the maximum allowed duration.

The property states that it is globally true that if angular roll accelerations are greater than the system's capability, then they must eventually not be greater than the capability within 10 time units (inclusive of the current time). This correctly captures 'shall not exceed capability for durations exceeding 10 time units', as it ensures that any period of exceeding capability must end within or at 10 time units.

The natural language expresses a bounded safety property: a specific condition (angular roll accelerations exceeding capability) must not persist for more than 10 time units. This can be translated as, 'Globally, if the angular roll accelerations are greater than the system's capability, then it must eventually become false within 10 time units (inclusive of the current time step).' This is captured by LTL template 14, where bool_exp2 is the condition that should not persist, and bool_exp3 is the negation of that condition, ensuring it eventually becomes false.

The property states that it is globally (G) always true that eventually (F) within 0 to 10 time units (inclusive of the current time), the condition "angular roll accelerations greater than the capability of the system (50deg/sec2)" will be false. This ensures that the angular roll accelerations do not exceed the capability for durations longer than 10 time units, as if they are true at any point, they must become false within the next 10 time units (including the current point), preventing them from holding for 11 or more consecutive time units.

The property states that globally, it must always be true that 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' eventually becomes false within a duration of N_DURATION time units. By setting N_DURATION to 10 and bool_exp3 to the negation of 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability', the formula ensures that the angular roll accelerations are not greater than the capability for durations exceeding 10 time units. If the condition 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' were to hold for 11 or more consecutive time units, the future operator F[0,10] would not find its negated form within that interval, thus violating the global condition.
